California
Fair POlitical
Practices Comrnission
Apri-I 6, 1990
Honorable Harriet U. StockwelI
Councilrnember
city of EI Caj on
581 Rin Rock Road
EI cajon, cA 92o2o
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-9 o-12 3
Dear Ms. stock$rell:
You have requested advice reqarding the apptication of the
conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the
trActtr) 1 to your duties as a member of the EI Cajon City CounciJ-.
The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your 1et-
ter and our telephone conversation of March 22 , t99o, which this
Ietter confirms.
OUESTION
Both you and your husband have purchased two plots in the EI
Cajon Cemetery which have a current fair rnarket value of 9900.
Under the Act, may you participate in decisions involving the
proposed extensj-on of Greenfield Drive and the expansion of the EI
Cajon Cemetery?
CONCLUS ION
Under the Act, you do not have an interest in real property
worth $1,ooo or more. Therefore, you nay participate in decisions
regarding the extension of Greenfietd Drive and the expansion of
the EI cajon cemetery.
FACTS
You are a rnernber of the Et cajon City Council. In 1985, you
and your husband purchased two plots in the EI Cajon cemetery for
$7oo. The fair market value of plots in the cemetery is now
$45o each. Therefore, the current fair market val-ue of your two
plots is 9900.
1 Gov"an^"nt code Sections
are to the Government code
81000-91015. A11 statutory references
unless otherwise indicated.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322-5660


FiIe No. A-90-12 3
Paqe 2
The councit is considering the proposed extension of
ereenfield Drive, which would cut across property EI cajon
cemetery would l-ike to use for expansion.
'ANAI.,YSI S
The Act prohibits a public official 
from making, participat-
ing in, or using her official 
position to influence a governmental
decision in which she knows or has reason to know she has a
financial interest. 
(Section 87100.) A public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public Aenerally, on the
official 
or a nember of her irnnediate fanily or on, among other
things:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or more.
(c) Any source of income, other than qifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution 
in the regular course of business on
terms available Lo the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol-
lars (9250) or more in value provided to, received
by or prornised to the public official 
within 12
months prior to the time $rhen the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
enployee, or holds any position of management.
(section 87103 (a) -(d) . )
As a menber of the EI cajon City Council, you are a public
official. 
(Section 82048.) Therefore you may not participate in
a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect on any real property in which you
have an interest of $1,ooo or more. (Section 87103(b).)
Section 82033 defines an trinterest in real propertyrr as:
... any leasehold, beneficial or ownership interest
or an option to acquire such an interest in real
property located in the jurisdiction 
oe/ned
directly, indirectly or beneficially by the public
official, 
or other filer, 
or his or her imnediate
family if the fair rnarket value of the interest is


File No. A-90-■23
Page 3
one thousand dollars (S1",000) or more....
(Emphasis added. )
Since the current fair market value of the two cemetery plots
is $9oo, you do not have an interest in real property worth $1,000
or more. Therefore, you may participate in decisions regarding
the proposed Greenfield Drive and EI cajon cenetery expansion.
However, should the fair market value of your two plots increase
to $1,ooo, please contact our office for a reconsideration of this
advice. At your request, I discussed this advice with your City
Attorney, Mr. McDougal, on March 26t L99o.
I trust this answers your questions. If you have any further
questions regarding this rnatter, please contact ne at (916)
322-590L.
S incere l-y ,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
